--- title: "EDA on Raw Messages" author: "Coco Yu" date: "2024-10-08" format: html: toc: true toc_depth: 4 embed-resources: true editor_options: chunk_output_type: console --- ## Code Status ## Notes ## Setup Chunk Defaults ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} knitr::opts_chunk$set(attr.output='style="max-height: 500px;"') ``` ::: Conflicts ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} options(conflicts.policy = "depends.ok") ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} library(tidyverse) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── ✔ dplyr 1.1.3 ✔ readr 2.1.4 ✔ forcats 1.0.0 ✔ stringr 1.5.0 ✔ ggplot2 3.4.3 ✔ tibble 3.2.1 ✔ lubridate 1.9.2 ✔ tidyr 1.3.0 ✔ purrr 1.0.2 ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() ✖ dplyr::lag() masks stats::lag() ℹ Use the conflicted package () to force all conflicts to become errors ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} library(kableExtra, exclude = "group_rows") library(lubridate) library(janitor, include.only = c("tabyl", "clean_names")) library(here, include.only = c("here")) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` here() starts at C:/Users/jyu274/Desktop/github/study_messages ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} theme_set(theme_classic()) ``` ::: Source Functions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} devtools::source_url("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/format_path.R?raw=true") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` ℹ SHA-1 hash of file is "a58e57da996d1b70bb9a5b58241325d6fd78890f" ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} devtools::source_url("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/fun_eda.R?raw=true") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` ℹ SHA-1 hash of file is "c045eee2655a18dc85e715b78182f176327358a7" ``` ::: ::: Absolute Paths ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} path_shared <- format_path(str_c("studydata/risk/data_processed/shared")) path_messages <- format_path(str_c("studydata/risk/data_processed/messages")) ``` ::: Read in Data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} # raw messages that have been cleaned raw_data <- read_csv( here(path_messages, "cleaned_messages.csv"), col_types = cols( subid = "d", address = "c", contact_type = "f", date = "T", type = "f", body = "c", msg_type = "f", phone_type = "f" ) ) # individual messages mapped on to prediction windows pred_3day <- read_csv( here(path_messages, "predictors/pred_3day.csv"), col_types = cols( subid = "d", day_start = "T", day_end = "T", lapse = "f", pred_onset = "T", address = "c", contact_type = "f", date = "T", type = "f", body = "c", msg_type = "f", phone_type = "f" ) ) pred_1week <- read_csv( here(path_messages, "predictors/pred_1week.csv"), col_types = cols( subid = "d", day_start = "T", day_end = "T", lapse = "f", pred_onset = "T", address = "c", contact_type = "f", date = "T", type = "f", body = "c", msg_type = "f", phone_type = "f" ) ) # concatenated text messages mapped on to prediction windows cat_3day <- read_csv( here(path_messages, "predictors/doc_3day.csv"), col_types = cols( subid = "d", day_start = "T", body = "c", lapse = "f" ) ) cat_1week <- read_csv( here(path_messages, "predictors/doc_1week.csv"), col_types = cols( subid = "d", day_start = "T", body = "c", lapse = "f" ) ) # labels labels <- read_csv(here(path_messages, "lapses.csv"), col_types = cols()) |> mutate(day_start = as_datetime(day_start, tz = "America/Chicago"), day_end = as_datetime(day_end, tz = "America/Chicago")) ``` ::: ## EDA on lapse labels > **Summary Table** > Conclusion 1: The total number of lapse labels in the dataset is 11562. > Conclusion 2: On average, each participant has 83.8 labels (sd = 11.4, median = 88, range = 30 - 90). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} (nrow(labels)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} [1] 11562 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} labels |> group_by(subid) |> summarize(n = n()) |> summarize( mean = mean(n), sd = sd(n), median = median(n), min = min(n), max = max(n) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} # A tibble: 1 × 5 mean sd median min max 1 83.8 11.4 88 30 90 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} labels |> group_by(subid) |> summarize(n = n()) |> ggplot(aes(x = n)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](eda_messages_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## EDA on raw messages In total, the study has 138 subjects. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} raw_data |> pull(subid) |> unique() |> length() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} [1] 138 ``` ::: ::: ### Message counts per participant > **Summary Table** > Conclusion 1: The total number of messages in the dataset is 313492. > Conclusion 2: On average, each subject has 2271.68 messages (sd = 2536.6, range = 100 - 15884). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} (nrow(raw_data)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} [1] 313492 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} raw_data |> group_by(subid) |> summarize(n = n()) |> summarize( mean = mean(n), sd = sd(n), median = median(n), min = min(n), max = max(n) ) |> print_kbl() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
mean sd median min max
2271.68 2536.6 1363.5 100 15884
````` ::: ::: **Histogram on # of messages per participant** ::: {#cell-fig-message_count .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-message_count #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Message Counts" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 raw_data |> group_by(subid) |> summarize(n = n()) |> ggplot(aes(x = n)) + geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "# of messages", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Message Counts](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-message_count-1.png){#fig-message_count width=336} ::: ::: ### Message Length per participant > **Summary Table** > Conclusion 1: The average message length is 10.4 (sd = 14.3, median = 7, range = 1 - 1266). > Conclusion 2: On average, each participant has a mean message length of 10.87 (sd = 3.97, range = 5.25 - 38.91) and a median message length of 7.14 (sd = 2.3, range = 4 - 19). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} # mean/median message length across all messages raw_data |> mutate(text_length = lengths(strsplit(raw_data$body, "\\s+"))) |> summarize( mean_length = mean(text_length), sd_length = sd(text_length), median_length = median(text_length), min_length = min(text_length), max_length = max(text_length) ) |> print_kbl() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
mean_length sd_length median_length min_length max_length
10.42 14.3 7 1 1266
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} # summary statistics for mean/median message length per participant raw_data |> mutate(text_length = lengths(strsplit(raw_data$body, "\\s+"))) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_length = mean(text_length), median_length = median(text_length), min_length = min(text_length), max_length = max(text_length) ) |> summarize( mean_mean_length = mean(mean_length), sd_mean_length = sd(mean_length), median_mean_length = median(mean_length), min_mean_length = min(mean_length), max_mean_length = max(mean_length), mean_median_length = mean(median_length), sd_median_length = sd(median_length), median_median_length = median(median_length), min_median_length = min(median_length), max_median_length = max(median_length) ) |> print_kbl() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
mean_mean_length sd_mean_length median_mean_length min_mean_length max_mean_length mean_median_length sd_median_length median_median_length min_median_length max_median_length
10.87 3.97 9.82 5.25 38.91 7.14 2.3 7 4 19
````` ::: ::: **Histogram on mean/median message length per participant** ::: {#cell-fig-message_length .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-message_length #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Mean Message Length" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 raw_data |> mutate(text_length = lengths(strsplit(raw_data$body, "\\s+"))) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize(mean_length = mean(text_length)) |> ggplot(aes(x = mean_length)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "mean message length", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Mean Message Length](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-message_length-1.png){#fig-message_length width=336} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} raw_data |> mutate(text_length = lengths(strsplit(raw_data$body, "\\s+"))) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize(median_length = median(text_length)) |> ggplot(aes(x = median_length)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "median message length", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](eda_messages_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## EDA on prediction windows prep: add in lapses that don't have any predictors ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_3day <- pred_3day |> full_join( labels, by = c("subid", "day_start", "day_end", "lapse") ) pred_1week <- pred_1week |> full_join( labels, by = c("subid", "day_start", "day_end", "lapse") ) cat_3day <- cat_3day |> full_join( labels, by = c("subid", "day_start", "lapse") ) cat_1week <- cat_1week |> full_join( labels, by = c("subid", "day_start", "lapse") ) ``` ::: sanity check: if percentage of missingness is the same for individual messages and concatenated messages ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} sum( pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(na = any(is.na(body))) |> pull(na) ) == nrow( cat_3day |> filter(is.na(body)) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} [1] TRUE ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} sum( pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(na = any(is.na(body))) |> pull(na) ) == nrow( cat_1week |> filter(is.na(body)) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} [1] TRUE ``` ::: ::: ### prediction window: 3-day > Conclusion 1: On average, each lapse label has 79.2 messages (sd = 112, median = 40, range = 1 - 1414) during the 3-day prediction window. > (**Missingness**) Conclusion 2: 11.9% of labels have no associated messages in the previous 3 days. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(n = n()) |> ungroup() |> summarize( mean = mean(n), sd = sd(n), median = median(n), min = min(n), max = max(n) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} # A tibble: 1 × 5 mean sd median min max 1 79.2 112. 40 1 1414 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(na = if_else(any(is.na(body)), 1, 0)) |> ungroup() |> summarize(misssing = sum(na) / n()) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} # A tibble: 1 × 1 misssing 1 0.119 ``` ::: ::: **Distribution by label** ::: {#cell-fig-message_count_3day .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-message_count_3day #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Message Counts in the 3-Day Prediction Window" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(n = n()) |> ggplot(aes(x = n)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "number of messages", y = "lapse counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Message Counts in the 3-Day Prediction Window](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-message_count_3day-1.png){#fig-message_count_3day width=336} ::: ::: > Conclusion 1: Each participant has an averaged 79.48 messages as predictors per label (sd = 87.12, median = 50.12, range = 2.97 - 529.45). > Conclusion 2: On average, each participant's data missingness is 12% (sd = .19, median = 1%, range = 0% - 77%). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize( n_messages = sum(!is.na(body)), missingness = sum(is.na(body)) / n() ) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_messages = mean(n_messages), median_messages = median(n_messages), mean_missing = mean(missingness), median_missing = median(missingness) ) |> summarize( mean_mean_messages = mean(mean_messages), sd_mean_messages = sd(mean_messages), median_mean_messages = median(mean_messages), min_mean_messages = min(mean_messages), max_mean_messages = max(mean_messages), mean_median_messages = mean(median_messages), sd_median_messages = sd(median_messages), median_median_messages = median(median_messages), min_median_messages = min(median_messages), max_median_messages = max(median_messages), mean_mean_missing = mean(mean_missing), sd_mean_missing = sd(mean_missing), median_mean_missing = median(mean_missing), min_mean_missing = min(mean_missing), max_mean_missing = max(mean_missing), mean_median_missing = mean(median_missing), sd_median_missing = sd(median_missing), median_median_missing = median(median_missing), min_median_missing = min(median_missing), max_median_missing = max(median_missing) ) |> print_kbl() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
mean_mean_messages sd_mean_messages median_mean_messages min_mean_messages max_mean_messages mean_median_messages sd_median_messages median_median_messages min_median_messages max_median_messages mean_mean_missing sd_mean_missing median_mean_missing min_mean_missing max_mean_missing mean_median_missing sd_median_missing median_median_missing min_median_missing max_median_missing
79.48 87.12 50.12 2.97 529.45 67.73 81.23 38 0 493 0.12 0.19 0.01 0 0.77 0.08 0.27 0 0 1
````` ::: ::: **Distribution by participant** ::: {#cell-fig-message_count_3day_byid .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-message_count_3day_byid #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Message Counts by Subject in the 3-Day Prediction Window" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize( n_messages = sum(!is.na(body)) ) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_messages = mean(n_messages) ) |> ggplot(aes(x = mean_messages)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "averaged number of messages per label", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Message Counts by Subject in the 3-Day Prediction Window](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-message_count_3day_byid-1.png){#fig-message_count_3day_byid width=336} ::: ::: ::: {#cell-fig-missingness_3day_byid .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-missingness_3day_byid #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Missingness by Subject in the 3-Day Prediction Window" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 pred_3day |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize( missingness = sum(is.na(body)) / n() ) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_missing = mean(missingness) ) |> ggplot(aes(x = mean_missing)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "percentage of missingness", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Missingness by Subject in the 3-Day Prediction Window](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-missingness_3day_byid-1.png){#fig-missingness_3day_byid width=336} ::: ::: ### prediction window: 1 week > Conclusion 1: On average, each lapse label has 180 messages (sd = 241, median = 96, range = 1 - 2866) during the 1-week prediction window. > (**Missingness**) Conclusion 2: 7.83% of labels have no associated messages in the previous week. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(n = n()) |> ungroup() |> summarize( mean = mean(n), sd = sd(n), median = median(n), min = min(n), max = max(n) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} # A tibble: 1 × 5 mean sd median min max 1 180. 241. 96 1 2866 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(na = if_else(any(is.na(body)), 1, 0)) |> ungroup() |> summarize(misssing = sum(na) / n()) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ```{style="max-height: 500px;"} # A tibble: 1 × 1 misssing 1 0.0783 ``` ::: ::: **Distribution by label** ::: {#cell-fig-message_count_1week .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-message_count_1week #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Message Counts in the 1-Week Prediction Window" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize(n = n()) |> ggplot(aes(x = n)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "number of messages", y = "lapse counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Message Counts in the 1-Week Prediction Window](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-message_count_1week-1.png){#fig-message_count_1week width=336} ::: ::: > Conclusion 1: Each participant has an averaged 181.23 messages as predictors per label (sd = 199.32, median = 113.47, range = 6.75 - 1200.76). > Conclusion 2: On average, each participant's data missingness is 8% (sd = .16, median = 0%, range = 0% - 73%). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize( n_messages = sum(!is.na(body)), missingness = sum(is.na(body)) / n() ) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_messages = mean(n_messages), median_messages = median(n_messages), mean_missing = mean(missingness), median_missing = median(missingness) ) |> summarize( mean_mean_messages = mean(mean_messages), sd_mean_messages = sd(mean_messages), median_mean_messages = median(mean_messages), min_mean_messages = min(mean_messages), max_mean_messages = max(mean_messages), mean_median_messages = mean(median_messages), sd_median_messages = sd(median_messages), median_median_messages = median(median_messages), min_median_messages = min(median_messages), max_median_messages = max(median_messages), mean_mean_missing = mean(mean_missing), sd_mean_missing = sd(mean_missing), median_mean_missing = median(mean_missing), min_mean_missing = min(mean_missing), max_mean_missing = max(mean_missing), mean_median_missing = mean(median_missing), sd_median_missing = sd(median_missing), median_median_missing = median(median_missing), min_median_missing = min(median_missing), max_median_missing = max(median_missing) ) |> print_kbl() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
mean_mean_messages sd_mean_messages median_mean_messages min_mean_messages max_mean_messages mean_median_messages sd_median_messages median_median_messages min_median_messages max_median_messages mean_mean_missing sd_mean_missing median_mean_missing min_mean_missing max_mean_missing mean_median_missing sd_median_missing median_median_missing min_median_missing max_median_missing
181.23 199.32 113.47 6.75 1200.76 168.09 193.22 96.5 0 1226 0.08 0.16 0 0 0.73 0.05 0.22 0 0 1
````` ::: ::: ::: {#cell-fig-message_count_1week_byid .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-message_count_1week_byid #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Message Counts by Subject in the 1-Week Prediction Window" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize( n_messages = sum(!is.na(body)) ) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_messages = mean(n_messages) ) |> ggplot(aes(x = mean_messages)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "averaged number of messages per label", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Message Counts by Subject in the 1-Week Prediction Window](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-message_count_1week_byid-1.png){#fig-message_count_1week_byid width=336} ::: ::: ::: {#cell-fig-missingness_1week_byid .cell} ```{.r .cell-code .hidden} #| label: fig-missingness_1week_byid #| fig-cap: "Distribution of Missingness by Subject in the 1-Week Prediction Window" #| fig-width: 3.5 #| fig-height: 2.5 pred_1week |> group_by(subid, day_start) |> summarize( missingness = sum(is.na(body)) / n() ) |> group_by(subid) |> summarize( mean_missing = mean(missingness) ) |> ggplot(aes(x = mean_missing)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#97C4C5") + labs(x = "percentage of missingness", y = "subject counts") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden} ``` `summarise()` has grouped output by 'subid'. You can override using the `.groups` argument. `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of Missingness by Subject in the 1-Week Prediction Window](eda_messages_files/figure-html/fig-missingness_1week_byid-1.png){#fig-missingness_1week_byid width=336} ::: :::
